auth_notes: Requires generating an API Key from the Busybusy settings/developer dashboard. The key is passed in the HTTP header as 'Authorization: Bearer [YOUR_TOKEN]'.
rate_limits: Standard rate limiting applies, typically around 100 requests per minute, though specific limits are often account-tier dependent.
gotchas: Pagination uses a limit/offset structure that requires manual loop configuration in n8n.
Timezone handling is critical as the API expects and returns UTC, requiring conversion for local reporting.
Filtering time entries requires precise ISO 8601 date strings.
client_must_provide: Admin access to the Busybusy dashboard to configure Webhook URLs
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Since there is no native node, a 'Header Auth' credential should be created in n8n. Webhooks are supported for real-time clock-in/out events but must be pointed to an n8n Webhook node URL manually.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Since there is no native node, a 'Header Auth' credential should be created in n8n. Webhooks are supported for real-time clock-in/out events but must be pointed to an n8n Webhook node URL manually.
